title: dactylorhiza euxina subsp. armeniaca
intro: Dactylorhiza euxina subsp. armeniaca, also known as Armenian marsh orchid, is a subspecies of the Dactylorhiza euxina orchid, native to the mountainous regions of Armenia and Georgia. It is a terrestrial orchid with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as offer cardiovascular protection and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with medications.
